posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked:
@William_Kelley rated Bollinger RD 2008 at 98+. I believe it’s getting released in the UK market next week at around £2400 per case.

I certainly have an eye on it. Was wondering if anyone else is planning to buy it.

posts.name: Bri@n Kelly
posts.cooked: Yup, I’ve already reached out to a few of my stores to secure an order. I was told it won’t be arriving at my local store until October in all likelihood. I am planning on hitting up the Bolly bar in London when in there in a few weeks to try it out.

posts.name: Tom Taylor
posts.cooked: Hitting the Connecticut market in roughly 3 weeks

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked: Do you know the US pricing?

posts.name: Tom Taylor
posts.cooked: I know of one offer for $350 at this point

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked: Ouch! $240+ for Bollinger? I’ve only had one in my life. Is it really expected to be better than CdC and DP?

posts.name: Rob Sutherland
posts.cooked: I find to my palate RD is much more special than DP. A little less special than p2 though.

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked:
I ask myself the same question. But frankly I felt the same when CDC had a big price hike in 2008.

As an aside, Grande Annee was released at £400/6 and is £900 / 6. So that makes RD look like a good value.

I am going to wake up tomorrow morning and decide, though I am pretty sure I will commit to it

posts.name: Whitmanlholt
posts.cooked: RD is the extended aged wine. It’s more akin to the DP P2 than the base vintage cuvee. Bollinger has a vintage wine that costs less (in the $150 range), as well as a very nice, widely distributed multivintage blend. They are generally richer, more oxidative-style wines. I enjoy the whole range, but the RDs are especially nice. In my view, $240+ is not unreasonable and fair given the current market prices for CdC and DP.

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked:
whitmanlholt:

They are generally richer, more oxidative-style wines.

Thanks. I definitely got that from my one experience. I think it’s not really for me.

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked: Interesting that 2008 comes in at a higher price point than what 2002 ($175) and 2007 ($150) is trading at.

posts.name: W_Shearer
posts.cooked: You’re being charged a premium for the ‘08 hype!

posts.name: Bri@n Kelly

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked:
These were the UK in-bond prices.

https://www.thewinesociety.com/product/champagne-bollinger-rd-2007

Re 2008 pricing , spoke to a couple of my trusted trade connections and here was their view (not facts)

“- some of the price increase is also not to be cheaper then the 2008 Grande Annee which is over £1000 per 6 bottles.
“

“- the demand is there for the 2008 and suspect the price will rise, too many emperor’s new clothes buyers out there and a lot of FOMO“. I thought they were talking about me

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal

I was about to pull the trigger last night but I am glad I decided to sleep on it.

Now thinking Bollinger RD 2002 or Grande Annee 2014 looks more interesting. Or CDC 2008.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked:
The price for '07 is £165, which is about $200. I am bringing a bottle of the '07 to a dinner tonight which will be my first time trying it.

I liked the '08 GA, but only had it once about 5 years ago. Lot’s of green apple and acidity, not what I typically associate with Bollinger.

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked: FWIW, I’ve never been a fan of the the RD (prefer the GA). I haven’t had one in well over a decade though so take that with a grain of salt.

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked:
How was it?

Ps: most of the UK retailers sent out offers for 2008 vintage today at $1440/6 (in-bond).

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked:
I thought it was meh. We started with a very good bottle of '96 DP, had an outstanding bottle of '96 Comtes mid-meal and finished with the RD. Showed some oxidative notes and red apple, but just did not impress. I much preferred the '14 GA I had a few months ago.

I saw the offer on the '08 and WK’s note is pretty positive.

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked:
Jon_Lawrence:

I thought it was meh. We started with a very good bottle of '96 DP, had an outstanding bottle of '96 Comtes mid-meal and finished with the RD. Showed some oxidative notes and red apple, but just did not impress. I much preferred the '14 GA I had a few months ago.

I saw the offer on the '08 and WK’s note is pretty positive.

Sounds like they haven’t changed much in the last decade

posts.name: Chris_Rickey
posts.cooked: WK gave the 2007 a 97, so basically the same as the 2008.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked: The '08 might be different though. The one time I had it it was not oxidative at all. Drank more like Salon than Selosse.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked: I saw that. Maybe it is shut-down now.

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal

posts.name: Joseph Grassa
posts.cooked:
Definitely paying for the hype around the vintage. BUT, the hype is not unwarranted. The 2008 La Grande Annee is my personal fav Champagne. I am far from a Champagne expert, and my preference leans heavily towards Bollinger stylistically. Personally I prefer LGA over DP, Cristal or CdC, but even when it comes to Bollinger’s LGA, the 2008 was a pretty clear favorite for me over the 2012 and 2014. Time will tell on the 2012 though. @William_Kelley thinks the 2012 may do better with age if I recall correctly.

Sadly, I have never had the RD Extra Brut and own only 1 bottle of the 2004, so unless I go buy some more, I do not foresee myself opening it too soon.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Chez Bollinger, I think 2008 is clearly > 2012 and 2014.

For Cristal, 2008 and 2012 are close and I’d vote for 2013 over 2008.

This choice with regards to Cristal has more to do with the producer’s evolution than the vintages themselves.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked:
Zachy’s special release pricing email today:

2008 R.D. - $425
2014 GA - $149

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked: GA is fine but RD seems to be priced very steeply.

posts.name: Eric Tomasi
posts.cooked: Also, I dont see a huge quality difference on good vintages of the RD vs GA. I’ve had the '90s side by side a couple times as well as the side by side 1996s and 2002s, and I only see huge differences early on and in weaker vintages. As they mature, I feel like they come closer. At $425, I’d much rather just get another 4 btls of '08 GA vs 2 bottles of the RD.

posts.name: Dinesh Goyal
posts.cooked: Heard in the next 4 weeks Bollinger will release a new disgorgement of RD 2008 with extended time on lees.
